lyrics:
"Ass hole crashed, tits up"

I found your texts, I know her name
You lied again, it's all the same
Your clothes are packed, they're at the door
Don't come crying anymore

I gave you love, I gave you trust
You threw it out, you killed us
Now I'm done, you had your fun
She deserves what you've become

It's tits up, you're outta luck
Took my heart and fucked it up
No more tears, no more fights
Get your shit and say goodbye-You're Tits Up

You swore to me, but then you lied
Now go crawl back to where she hides
Don't send your friends, don't waste my time
You made your choice, now pay the fine

I gave you love, I gave you trust
You threw it out, you killed us
Now I'm done, you had your fun
She deserves what you've become

It's tits up, you're outta luck
Took my heart and fucked it up
No more tears, no more fights
Get your shit and say goodbye-You're Tits Up

Don't call me drunk, I've blocked your calls
I don't forgive, I won't go back
You burned it down, you rotten slag
Go sleep with the mess you laid

It's tits up, you're outta luck
Took my heart and fucked it up
No more tears, no more fights
Get your shit and say goodbye-You're Tits Up

Locked the door, changed the key
You're just a ghost to me
And If you think I'm sad and crying
Imagine me tits down on your best friend
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a dense, aggressive texture built on heavily distorted guitars and a driving rhythm section, establishing a confrontational and defiant atmosphere. The energy is consistently high and explosive, with a feel that is pure pop-punk angst, fueled by powerful, shouted female vocals that convey a strong sense of betrayal and empowerment. The production features a modern "wall of sound" approach, creating a unified and powerful front, yet the lead vocal cuts through the dense mix with impressive clarity and attitude. A defining quality is the incredibly catchy and anthemic chorus hook, "It's tits up," which delivers a memorable, rebellious punch that solidifies the song's identity.

review.review_text: Running at a breathless 172 BPM in E minor, 'Tits Up' is the kind of breakup song that doesn't ask for sympathy, it demands the exit. The distorted guitar wall and punchy rhythm section hit immediately, but it's the chorus that earns its keep: 'Took my heart and fucked it up' lands with the blunt economy of early Alanis Morissette, and the final twist, 'imagine me tits down on your best friend,' is the line that'll stick with you long after the 3:02 runtime. PCBender keeps the production loud and unified without burying the vocal, which cuts through with real attitude. A clean, catchy gut-punch of a track.
anchors_used: chorus lyric 'Took my heart and fucked it up'
closing lyric 'imagine me tits down on your best friend'
